creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
Thank you for the update.  This looks pretty good!  Here are some issues I found:

 - when I try to build the port with option NOGRAPHIC, it still tries to link
   against SDL.  Specifically, "make stage-qa" reports:

====> Running Q/A tests (stage-qa)
Error: /usr/local/bin/nll is linked to /usr/local/lib/libSDL2-2.0.so.0 from devel/sdl20 but it is not declared as a dependency
Warning: you need USE_SDL+=sdl2

   This can also be seen in the build log which has

cc   main.o -O -Wall -g  -L. -lnll   -L/usr/local/lib -lreadline     -lcurses  `/usr/local/bin/sdl2-config --libs`     -lm  -o nll

   despite SDL2 being turned off supposedly.  This also applies when SDL1 is
   selected as the backend.

 - on the contrary, if NOGRAPHIC is selected and SDL is not installed, the
   build fails:

--- audio.i ---
audio.c:13:10: fatal error: 'SDL2/SDL.h' file not found
#include <SDL2/SDL.h>
         ^~~~~~~~~~~~
1 error generated.
*** [audio.i] Error code 1

 - For ports that have the option to support graphics output, it may be useful
   to provide flavors, so binary packages both with and without graphics will be 
   built.  Otherwise users wishing to install non-graphics when graphics are the
   default would have to build from source.  Please check if it makes sense to
   use flavors for this port and if so, define appropriate flavors.

 - SDL1 is very old and subsumed by SDL2.  While it is ok to have both, it may
   be enough to only have support for SDL2.

Please check these issues and resubmit.  The port can likely be committed once these are addressed.
